name: Optimization & Prescriptive Engine
description: Optimization system that takes business constraints, objectives, and real-time data to compute optimal decisions. Uses mathematical optimization (linear programming, constraint satisfaction) combined with ML-based scoring to recommend actions like optimal routes, pricing, and resource allocation.
logisticsContext: Optimization engines drive route planning, container load optimization, dynamic pricing, warehouse slotting, carrier allocation, and fleet scheduling. Suboptimal recommendations directly increase costs and reduce service levels.

nodes.label: Business Rules Engine
nodes.description: Encodes hard constraints, SLA requirements, regulatory limits, and operational policies that all candidate solutions must satisfy. Rules include maximum transit times, hazmat restrictions, weight limits, driver hours-of-service regulations, carrier contract terms, and customer-specific delivery windows. This is the authoritative source of truth for what constitutes a feasible solution.
vulnerabilities: Rule tampering through unauthorized configuration changes that relax safety or compliance constraints
Inconsistent rule versions across environments causing different optimization outcomes in staging vs production
Missing constraint coverage for edge cases such as new trade lanes, seasonal restrictions, or recently enacted regulations
Rule injection via external integrations that supply constraint parameters from untrusted sources

nodes.label: Objective Function
nodes.description: Defines the mathematical optimization goal — typically a weighted combination of cost minimization, service level maximization, asset utilization, and carbon footprint reduction. Weights are tuned by operations leadership and encode business priorities. The objective function translates strategic goals into a computable scoring formula that the constraint solver maximizes or minimizes.
vulnerabilities: Weight manipulation that silently shifts optimization priorities to favor specific carriers, routes, or pricing outcomes
Objective function drift where weights become stale and no longer reflect current business strategy
Multi-objective conflicts producing Pareto-suboptimal solutions when cost and service level targets are poorly balanced
Lack of audit trail for weight changes making it impossible to trace when and why optimization behavior shifted

nodes.label: Real-time Data Feeds
nodes.description: Ingests live data from external sources including GPS telemetry, weather APIs, traffic services, port congestion feeds, carrier capacity APIs, fuel price indices, and spot market rates. This data provides the current-state inputs that the optimizer needs to produce relevant recommendations. Data quality and freshness directly impact recommendation accuracy.
vulnerabilities: Data poisoning through compromised or spoofed GPS, weather, or traffic feeds that steer the optimizer toward suboptimal routes
Stale data from API outages or caching failures causing the optimizer to plan against outdated conditions
Rate feed manipulation where a malicious carrier API reports false capacity or pricing to gain allocation advantage
Denial of service through feed flooding that overwhelms ingestion pipelines and degrades optimization timeliness
Man-in-the-middle attacks on unencrypted feed connections altering transit times or capacity figures in flight

nodes.label: Historical Data Store
nodes.description: Repository of past optimization decisions and their real-world outcomes — actual transit times vs predicted, realized costs vs estimated, service level hits and misses, carrier performance records, and seasonal demand patterns. Used by the ML scoring model for training and by the performance monitor for accuracy tracking. Contains years of operational intelligence.
vulnerabilities: Training data poisoning through injection of fabricated historical records that skew model predictions
Data retention policy violations if historical records contain PII or competitively sensitive pricing not properly governed
Schema drift when data format changes break downstream model training pipelines without detection
Survivorship bias if only successful optimizations are recorded, causing models to miss failure patterns

nodes.label: Constraint Solver
nodes.description: Mathematical optimization engine (OR-Tools, Gurobi, CPLEX, or custom solver) that finds feasible solutions satisfying all hard constraints from the business rules engine while optimizing the objective function. Handles linear programming, mixed-integer programming, constraint satisfaction, and vehicle routing problems. Produces a ranked set of candidate solutions within computational time budgets.
vulnerabilities: Solver timeout exploitation where adversarial input complexity forces fallback to inferior heuristic solutions
License server dependency creating single points of failure for commercial solvers like Gurobi or CPLEX
Numerical instability in edge cases producing solutions that appear feasible but violate constraints at execution
Supply chain risk if solver library updates introduce regressions or backdoors in optimization algorithms

nodes.label: ML Scoring Model
nodes.description: Machine learning model that predicts real-world outcomes for candidate solutions generated by the constraint solver. Estimates actual transit times, delivery success probability, carrier reliability scores, and cost overrun likelihood based on historical patterns and current conditions. Augments mathematical optimization with learned patterns that are difficult to encode as explicit constraints.
vulnerabilities: Model poisoning through corrupted training data that systematically biases predictions toward specific carriers or routes
Adversarial inputs crafted to exploit model blind spots and produce artificially high scores for suboptimal solutions
Concept drift as market conditions change faster than model retraining cycles, degrading prediction accuracy
Model supply chain compromise if pre-trained components or embeddings are sourced from untrusted repositories
Lack of explainability making it difficult to audit why certain solutions receive inflated or deflated scores

nodes.label: Solution Evaluator
nodes.description: Combines constraint solver output with ML scoring model predictions to rank, filter, and select the best candidate solutions. Applies business logic filters (minimum confidence thresholds, diversity requirements, risk limits) and produces a final ranked recommendation set. Implements guardrails that reject solutions failing sanity checks even if they score well mathematically.
vulnerabilities: Ranking manipulation if evaluation weights are modified to consistently favor specific outcomes or vendors
Guardrail bypass when edge-case solutions pass individual checks but fail holistic reasonableness tests
Insufficient diversity in recommendations creating over-reliance on a single carrier, route, or strategy
Threshold misconfiguration rejecting viable solutions or accepting risky ones due to poorly calibrated cutoffs

nodes.label: Decision API
nodes.description: RESTful or gRPC API layer that serves optimization recommendations to consuming business systems — TMS, WMS, pricing engines, booking platforms, and dispatch systems. Handles request authentication, rate limiting, response formatting, and SLA enforcement. Provides both synchronous (real-time pricing) and asynchronous (batch route planning) recommendation endpoints.
vulnerabilities: API authentication bypass allowing unauthorized systems to consume or influence optimization recommendations
Response tampering if API responses are not integrity-protected, allowing man-in-the-middle modification of recommendations
Rate limit exhaustion through automated queries that consume solver capacity and delay legitimate optimization requests
Information disclosure through verbose API error messages revealing solver internals, constraint structures, or pricing logic

nodes.label: Operations Dashboard
nodes.description: Web-based interface where dispatchers, planners, and operations managers review optimization recommendations before execution. Displays recommended routes, load plans, pricing suggestions, and carrier allocations alongside confidence scores, cost breakdowns, and alternative options. Supports manual override, approval workflows, and what-if scenario analysis.
vulnerabilities: Social engineering through manipulated dashboard displays that make suboptimal recommendations appear optimal
Override abuse where authorized users consistently override optimization to favor specific carriers for kickback arrangements
Session hijacking granting unauthorized access to recommendation approval workflows
Cross-site scripting if recommendation labels or carrier names containing malicious payloads render unsanitized in the dashboard

nodes.label: Execution System
nodes.description: Applies approved optimization decisions by triggering downstream actions — booking carriers, dispatching vehicles, updating pricing in quoting systems, assigning warehouse slots, and generating load plans. Bridges the gap between recommendation and action. Implements rollback capabilities for decisions that fail during execution.
vulnerabilities: Unauthorized execution of recommendations that bypassed human approval workflows
Replay attacks re-executing previously approved decisions under changed conditions
Partial execution failures leaving the system in an inconsistent state (e.g., carrier booked but dispatch not updated)
Rollback failures when downstream systems do not support transactional reversal of applied decisions

nodes.label: Performance Monitor
nodes.description: Continuously tracks how optimization recommendations compare to actual outcomes — predicted vs actual transit times, estimated vs realized costs, planned vs actual service levels. Detects model drift, data quality degradation, and systematic biases. Generates alerts when recommendation accuracy drops below thresholds and feeds performance data back to model retraining pipelines.
vulnerabilities: Alert suppression through tampering with monitoring thresholds that masks declining recommendation quality
Metric gaming when outcome data is selectively reported to inflate apparent optimization accuracy
Delayed detection if monitoring operates on batch cycles while recommendation quality degrades in real time
Feedback loop poisoning where manipulated performance data corrupts the retraining pipeline for the ML scoring model
